title: competent-search-thing plugin manifest
description: One plugin's manifest.json, loaded from <configDir>/plugins/<dir>/manifest.json. Source of truth: internal/plugin/manifest.go and trigger.go. The loader ignores unknown keys and fills defaults (v=1, name=id, timeout_ms=1500, bangs=[id]); this schema is stricter than the loader on purpose so editors flag mistakes. Reachability rule (described, not encoded): a plugin must be reachable somehow -- omitting 'bangs' defaults it to [id], but an explicitly empty 'bangs' list REQUIRES a 'trigger', otherwise the manifest is rejected as unreachable.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema. Ignored by the app.

v.description: Manifest version; must be 1 (omitting it means 1).

id.description: Unique plugin id: 1-32 chars of [a-z0-9_-], starting with a letter or digit. The built-in ids 'bangs', 'app' and 'apps' are taken. When two manifests declare the same id, the alphabetically-first directory wins.

name.description: Display name, shown as the section header and in the bang chip. Defaults to the id.

type.description: The transport: 'command' runs a subprocess per query (request JSON on stdin, response JSON on stdout); 'http' POSTs the request JSON to a URL.

items.description: A bang name (same syntax as a plugin id; lowercase).

bangs.description: Bang names targeting this plugin ('!calc 2+2'). Omitted means [id]. Names are lowercased and deduped on load. An explicit [] means 'no bangs' and then 'trigger' is required (a plugin with neither is rejected as unreachable).

context.description: App-context parts sent with every request. Undeclared parts are never sent; duplicates are deduped by the loader (this schema rejects them).

timeout_ms.description: Per-query time budget in milliseconds. 0 or omitted means the default 1500. The loader CLAMPS out-of-range values to 100..10000 instead of rejecting; this schema rejects them so authors notice.

allow_run_command.description: Must be true for this plugin's results to carry run_command actions; otherwise any result with one is dropped by the sanitizer.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: When the plugin sees untargeted (non-bang) queries. The plugin matches when ANY text path (prefix, regex, all_queries -- tried in that order, first match decides the stripped query) matches AND the focused_app gate, when present, matches. Bang targeting bypasses triggers entirely. Omit the whole trigger to make the plugin bang-only.

prefix.description: Case-insensitive (rune-folded) prefix match on the typed query; the remainder, trimmed, becomes the request's 'stripped'.

regex.description: Case-insensitive RE2 pattern matched against the RAW query; on match 'stripped' is the trimmed raw query. An invalid pattern rejects the manifest at load.

all_queries.description: Match every query. When set and min_query_len is 0, the effective minimum stripped length is 2, so an all-queries plugin does not fire on single keystrokes.

min_query_len.description: Minimum STRIPPED query length in runes, gating ALL text paths. 0 means no minimum (except the all_queries default of 2, see above).

debounce_ms.description: Extra delay before dispatch; a newer keystroke cancels the wait, so a debounced plugin only sees queries the user paused on. The dispatcher CLAMPS values to 0..2000; this schema rejects out-of-range values.

focused_boost.description: Added to every result score (result scores are clamped at 100) when the focused_app gate matches -- lets app-specific plugins outrank generic ones. The dispatcher CLAMPS values to 0..100; this schema rejects out-of-range values.

focusedGate.title: Focused-app gate
focusedGate.description: The trigger only matches when the application focused at hotkey press matches (case-insensitive RE2). At least one pattern must be non-empty (both empty rejects the manifest at load); an empty or omitted pattern is a wildcard. When no focused app is known (Wayland, degraded platforms) the gate never matches (fail-closed).

name_regex.description: Case-insensitive RE2 matched against the focused app's name (e.g. 'firefox|chrome').

exe_regex.description: Case-insensitive RE2 matched against the focused app's executable path.

commandSpec.title: Command transport
commandSpec.description: Required for type 'command'. The app runs argv directly (no shell), request JSON on stdin (then closed), response JSON from stdout (capped 1 MiB). An absolute argv[0] runs as-is; one containing a path separator resolves relative to the manifest's directory; a bare name goes through PATH. The working directory is always the manifest's directory.

argv.description: The command line; at least one entry, none empty.

httpSpec.title: HTTP transport
httpSpec.description: Required for type 'http'. The app POSTs the request JSON (Content-Type: application/json) to url and reads the response JSON from a 2xx reply (body capped 1 MiB). Redirects are followed at most 3 hops and only to http(s) targets.

url.description: Absolute http or https URL with a host.

headers.description: Headers set on every request (e.g. an API key).
